sections.heading: Welcome
sections.content: VibeScholar is an opinionated research-operations platform that helps you plan, execute, and track AI/ML research projects from idea to publication. This is the minimal (zero) release with one starter project.

sections.heading: What is VibeScholar?
sections.content:
VibeScholar organizes every research project into five pillars:

1. **Overview** — Structured project definition with motivation, scope, questions, and risks
2. **Execution** — Phased Vibe Coding (VC) and Vibe Research (VR) steps with AI prompts and acceptance criteria
3. **Timeline** — Milestone-based tracking with Kanban view and deadline management
4. **Artifacts** — Registry of code, datasets, figures, tables, and reports linked to milestones
5. **Literature** — Imported papers with tags, summaries, and normalized metadata

steps.label: Create Your First Project
steps.description: Use the dashboard to create a new project. Give it a name, definition, and domain tags.

steps.label: Write an Overview
steps.description: Open your project and write a structured overview with Definition, Motivation, Scope, Questions, and Risks.

steps.label: Add Execution Steps
steps.description: Create Vibe Coding steps for implementation work and Vibe Research steps for writing and analysis.

steps.label: Set Milestones
steps.description: Add milestones with deadlines and acceptance criteria. Track progress in the Kanban view.

steps.label: Import Literature
steps.description: Import papers via CSV or JSONL. Tag them for filtering and reference in your research steps.

items.description: Implementation-focused steps — code, tests, infrastructure, packaging.
Research-focused steps — literature review, experimental design, analysis, writing.
Steps are grouped into phases 0-5: Setup, Data, Core, Baselines, Experiments, Finalization.
A structured project specification that auto-generates execution steps, milestones, and artifacts.
Red/Amber/Green health indicator per project based on progress and blockers.

items.text: Create your first project from the dashboard
Write a structured overview with at least 3 sections
Import at least 5 papers into the literature panel
